name: cupid-hostapd
title: cupid-hostapd
summary: Fork of hostapd to exploit hertbleed vulnerability on wireless networks
description:
Fork of hostapd to exploit hertbleed vulnerability on wireless networks
cupid-hostapd provide a binary of the same name that has been patched
to exploit the heartbleed vulnerability over EAP TLS tunneled protocols
(EAP-PEAP, EAP-TLS, EAP-TTLS) in use in wireless networks.
.
With cupid-hostapd you can setup a fake wireless network to exploit
the vulnerability of terminals that try to connect to it.
.
Please see presentation slides for a simple introduction to cupid:
http://www.slideshare.net/lgrangeia

arguments.description: Include key data in debug messages.
arguments.raw: -K  Include key data in debug messages.

arguments.description: Include timestamps in some debug messages.
arguments.raw: -t  Include timestamps in some debug messages.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cupid-hostapd because Fork of hostapd to exploit hertbleed vulnerability on wireless networks.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cupid-hostapd as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cupid-hostapd with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cupid-hostapd --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: hostapd - IEEE 802.11 AP, IEEE 802.1X/WPA/WPA2/EAP/RADIUS Authenticator.
